topic: On the confounded complete-mediation graph X->M->Y (X randomized, M<->Y confounded by an unmeasured U, all binary), characterize when mediator monotonicity M_1>=M_0 strictly tightens the SHARP sign-free response-function bound on the probability of necessity PN=P(Y_{x=0}=0 | X=1,Y=1).

open_problems.open_problem: For binary complete mediation with randomized X and arbitrary M-Y confounding, characterize by a necessary-and-sufficient observable condition when deleting mediator-defier types M(1)<M(0) strictly tightens either sharp endpoint of PN=P(Y(0)=0 | X=1,Y=1).

source_refs.claim: Its mediator-PC analysis assumes no direct effect and no mediator-outcome confounding, leaving the confounded complete-mediation case outside its sharp-bound result.
source_refs.author: A. P. Dawid, R. Murtas, M. Musio

source_refs.claim: It identifies mediated probabilities under strong cross-world ignorability and identifies weakening those assumptions through bounds or sensitivity analysis as a remaining direction.
source_refs.author: M. Rubinstein, M. Cuellar, D. Malinsky

source_refs.claim: It gives sharp bounds for PN/PNS/PS under general data assumptions, but not the response-type restriction from a confounded observed mediator plus mediator monotonicity.
source_refs.author: J. Tian, J. Pearl
source_refs.venue: Annals of Mathematics and Artificial Intelligence

source_refs.excerpt: Pivot Conjecture 2 into a standalone proxy-response polytope theorem... without relying on bridge-null loadings.
does not fully construct a complete finite-support law showing strict containment of the whole effect interval

open_problems.why_unsolved: The mediation-PC papers either remove the M-Y confounding difficulty or replace it with cross-world ignorability; generic PN bounds do not say which mediator response-type faces are affected. The in-repo proxy attempt also found that its bridge-null loading was forced to vanish by row-span geometry, so a strict gain cannot be inferred from that surrogate mechanism. The missing result is a direct analysis of the full response-type polytope: a mediator-defier deletion matters only if it excludes an endpoint-attaining coupling, and a full finite-support witness is required to prove that this occurs on an open observable regime.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp finite-SCM response-type theorem giving max/min-of-affine endpoints under unrestricted and monotone mediator types, an iff threshold T(P) for L1>L0 and/or U1<U0, and a closed-form positive endpoint-gap formula on a nonempty open compatible set.

writing_exemplar.why: Mirror its causes-of-effects motivation and its transition from experimental PN bounds to the information contributed by an observed mediator.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: experimental treatment-outcome data bound individual attribution -> observing an intermediate mechanism can sharpen those bounds -> what remains sharp when that mediator is outcome-confounded but treatment-to-mediator monotone?

method_exemplars.technique: Encode deterministic binary potential-response types as a simplex, impose all interventional-cell equalities and the monotonicity zero restrictions, then use LP primal/dual vertex arguments to prove sharp envelopes.
method_exemplars.why_closest: PN and the mediator-defier restriction are respectively a linear objective and a face deletion in the same finite response-type geometry needed for T(P).

method_exemplars.technique: Derive the Hadamard directional derivative of the finite max/min endpoint map and use a derivative-aware bootstrap at ties rather than a smooth delta method.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The requested endpoints and their gap are nonsmooth max/min affine plug-in functionals at exactly the active-face transitions relevant to strictness.

open_problems.open_problem: Using every cell of the full randomized law P(M,Y|do(X)), determine the sharp sign-free and mediator-monotone PN intervals and characterize which cross-arm mediator-outcome cells are facet-essential for each endpoint.

source_refs.excerpt: the formula only uses the Z=0,D=0 mixture and omits the Z=1,D=0 observed cell, which ... can tighten the bound.
the sharp identified set ... is the interval obtained by ... bounding [a] latent ... mean inside [an] observed mixture.

open_problems.why_unsolved: The adjacent bounded-defier proposal was rejected because an apparently sharp expression discarded an observed treatment-arm cell constraining the same latent mixture. In the mediated PN problem, conditioning on X=1,Y=1 makes a selected stratum tempting, but sharpness requires projection under every equality from P(M,Y|do(x)). No archived artifact supplies the active-facet map that distinguishes cells redundant for a PN endpoint from cells that exclude a would-be response-type extremum.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp identified-set theorem expressing [L0,U0] and [L1,U1] as finite max/min affine functionals of all eight P(M=m,Y=y|do(x)) cells, with an active-facet characterization of redundant and endpoint-tightening cells.

writing_exemplar.why: It provides a close motivation arc for sharp causal bounds with a confounded intermediate variable.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: randomization reveals treatment-arm distributions -> mediator-outcome confounding leaves cross-world uncertainty -> every observed cell constrains latent response types -> derive sharp PN rather than a selected-cell mixture bound

method_exemplars.technique: Project the complete observed-data constraint system over a finite latent response-type simplex and certify sharpness by latent-type completion.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The required correction is exactly to enforce every randomized mediator-outcome cell before optimizing the PN functional.

method_exemplars.technique: Apply directional-delta inference to finite maxima and minima of plug-in affine cell-probability coordinates.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The full-law endpoint formulas are piecewise affine and change active facets at probability ties.

open_problems.open_problem: Establish uniformly valid inference for plug-in sharp PN bounds in the randomized binary confounded-mediator model when max/min affine pieces tie or mediator monotonicity changes the active response-type face.

source_refs.claim: It develops inference for parameters that are only directionally differentiable, including convex partially identified sets, where naive bootstrap methods can fail.
source_refs.author: Z. Fang, A. Santos

source_refs.claim: It develops inference for bounds defined by lower and upper envelopes, but not the PN-normalized active-face geometry of a confounded-mediation response-type polytope.
source_refs.author: V. Chernozhukov, S. Lee, A. M. Rosen

source_refs.claim: It obtains sharp symbolic bounds through finite canonical confounder states for mediator effects, but its targets are risk-difference decompositions rather than PN endpoint inference.
source_refs.author: E. E. Gabriel, M. C. Sachs, A. Sjölander
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

open_problems.why_unsolved: Response-function enumeration makes each endpoint piecewise affine in the randomized cell law rather than globally differentiable. At equality of two pieces—the regime where a mediator-defier restriction begins or ceases to bind—the ordinary delta method and ordinary n-out-of-n bootstrap need not deliver valid coverage. General directional-delta and intersection-bound results exist, but none of the scanned sharp-mediation work specializes them to the PN normalization, joint endpoint interval, and active-face changes generated by this confounded complete-mediation polytope.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A directional-differentiability and limit-law theorem plus a computable derivative-aware bootstrap or calibrated confidence-set procedure with uniform asymptotic coverage for [L0,U0], [L1,U1], and their width gap.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the closest sharp-bounds mediation template and cleanly motivates canonical finite latent-state representations under mediator-outcome confounding.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: randomization identifies total effects but not mediator-confounded mechanisms -> finite response types give sharp symbolic bounds -> how should valid uncertainty attach to a sharp PN envelope with nonsmooth active faces?

method_exemplars.technique: Compute the endpoint map's Hadamard directional derivative and use the modified bootstrap for nonsmooth maps instead of bootstrapping a nonexistent ordinary derivative.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Finite maximum/minimum affine PN endpoints are directly directionally differentiable but nonsmooth at ties.

method_exemplars.technique: Use calibrated critical values and bias correction for lower and upper envelopes to construct confidence regions for a sharp identified set.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Selecting PN endpoint pieces is an LP-envelope analogue of their intersection-bound problem.

literature_map:
Tian and Pearl (2000, Annals of Mathematics and Artificial Intelligence; arXiv:1301.3898) gives sharp general bounds for PN/PNS/PS.
Dawid, Murtas, and Musio (2014, arXiv:1411.2636) studies PC/PN with a complete mediator, but under no mediator-outcome confounding.
Murtas, Dawid, and Musio (2017, arXiv:1706.04857) develops further complete/partial-mediation PC bounds and shows mediator information can fail to tighten a bound.
Dawid, Humphreys, and Musio (2024, Sociological Methods & Research; DOI 10.1177/00491241211036161; arXiv:1907.00399) studies chains of complete mediators under a no-confounding process model.
Sjölander (2009, Statistics in Medicine; DOI 10.1002/sim.3493) shows M-Y confounding defeats direct-effect identification even under randomized treatment and gives average-effect bounds.
Gabriel, Sachs, and Sjölander (2023, JASA; DOI 10.1080/01621459.2022.2057316; arXiv:2112.13632) gives canonical-confounder sharp bounds for binary mediator decompositions, not individual-attribution PN.
Kawakami and Tian (2025, AAAI; DOI 10.1609/aaai.v39i25.34886; arXiv:2412.14491) identifies direct/indirect PNS under sequential ignorability and structural monotonicity.
Rubinstein, Cuellar, and Malinsky (2025, Journal of Causal Inference; DOI 10.1515/jci-2024-0019) defines mediated probabilities of causation and flags the strength of cross-world ignorability.
The live tension is sharp individual PN under weak assumptions versus mediated-PC point identification under cross-world assumptions.
Balke and Pearl (1997, JASA; DOI 10.1080/01621459.1997.10474074) supplies the finite response-function/LP sharpness template.
Fang and Santos (2019, ReStud; DOI 10.1093/restud/rdy049) supplies derivative-aware inference for nonsmooth directionally differentiable maps.
Chernozhukov, Lee, and Rosen (2013, Econometrica; DOI 10.3982/ECTA8718) supplies envelope/intersection-bound inference.
No scanned paper supplied a necessary-and-sufficient observable condition for the strict gain from deleting mediator defiers in a confounded complete-mediation PN LP.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined the adjacent active qid scm_mediator_monotonicity_pn_v1; it contains run state only, with no prior proposal or review files.
Mined failed pid_mediation_proxy_v1, including its proposal, rejected angle, README, and reviewer JSONs.
Its reviewer found a row-span/null-space collapse: the bridge-null mechanism cannot tighten the target and prescribed a direct response-type-polytope pivot.
Mined failed pid_late_bounded_defiers_one_sided_compliance; its README records a claimed sharp mixture formula that omitted an observed arm cell capable of tightening the bound.
Mined candidate pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 and its reviews; they require a complete-law finite-support witness and perturbation-stable strict gap, not only local algebra.
No parent upgrade_from artifacts were supplied.
